item.title: Sega Creates Life-Sized Sonic with Synthetic DNA for 35th Anniversary
item.description:
Visitors at Sega 's Tokyo store location will soon get a chance to see a life-sized statue of Sonic the Hedgehog that contains synthetic DNA. Since debuting in 1991, Sonic has been Sega's main mascot, quickly becoming an international icon. The blue blur has remained relevant thanks to a steady stream of video games, TV shows, movies, comics, and more. There are few characters in gaming that have managed to remain as popular over the years, and Sonic helped make Sega a household name.

2026 marks the 35th anniversary of Sonic the Hedgehog , and Sega is celebrating the occasion in a number of different ways. New DLC was revealed for Sonic Racing: CrossWorlds at Summer Game Fest, which will add content based on Godzilla and Evangelion . Sega also pulled back the curtain on a new spin-off game called Sonic Pico Park .

Synthetic DNA in a Sonic Statue

New video game content is unsurprising for the anniversary of a major gaming icon, but Sega's collaboration with LOM BABY is certainly unique. Using biotechnology, LOM BABY has created an artificial DNA strand made up of all the core elements of Sonic the Hedgehog . These core elements include Sonic's spin dash, his heroism, and even his struggles with water. That strand was then placed inside a green Chaos Emerald within the transparent chest of the statue. LOM BABY has done similar art collaborations with gaming companies, including a recreation of the Resident Evil T-Virus earlier this year.

Who’s That Character?

Identify the silhouettes before time runs out.

5.0 s
0 / 10

Results

0

High Score: 0

—

More Games
Who’s That Character? Identify the silhouettes before time runs out.
Easy (7.5s)Medium (5.0s)Hard (2.5s)Permadeath (2.5s)
5.0 s
0 / 10
Results
0
High Score: 0 —
More Games

It's unclear whether Sega will bring the LOM BABY collaboration to other countries, or if this will remain exclusive to Japan. The statue can be found in Sega's Tokyo Store location starting on June 23. That's just two days after the 35th anniversary of the original Sonic the Hedgehog game's release on the Sega Genesis, so the timing lines up fairly well. The whole thing is pretty unusual, but it should make for an interesting display piece for Sonic fans that happen to be in the area.

While 2026 marks a significant milestone for the character, it seems like next year could be even bigger for the franchise. Sonic the Hedgehog 4 comes to theaters in 2027, continuing Paramount's highly-successful film franchise. The movie will seemingly put a big focus on Amy Rose and Metal Sonic, two characters that made their big-screen debut at the end of Sonic the Hedgehog 3 . The movies have helped introduce a lot of new fans to the franchise, and Sega has taken advantage by releasing new games to coincide. That's exactly what happened in 2024, when Sonic x Shadow Generations was released on multiple platforms around the same time Sonic the Hedgehog 3 was in theaters.

Find the odd one out before the timer hits zero.

Round 1

Which one doesn't belong?

0 / 10
10.0 s

Results

0/5

Best: 0/5

—

More Games
Find the odd one out before the timer hits zero.
Easy (15s)Medium (10s)Hard (5s)Permadeath (5s)
Round 1 Which one doesn't belong?
0 / 10
10.0 s
Results
0/5
Best: 0/5 —
More Games

Sega's plans for future games are a mystery at the moment, but a Sonic CD remake has been rumored. Amy and Metal Sonic made their debuts in that game, so the timing could line up with the theatrical arrival of Sonic the Hedgehog 4 . Sonic Racing: CrossWorlds will also continue to see support in 2027, and the developers have indicated that there might be even more characters and tracks added if there's enough demand from players. The Sonic series might be turning 35 this year, but it doesn't seem to be slowing down as it gets older.

Source: Sega PR
item.content:encoded:
Visitors at Sega 's Tokyo store location will soon get a chance to see a life-sized statue of Sonic the Hedgehog that contains synthetic DNA. Since debuting in 1991, Sonic has been Sega's main mascot, quickly becoming an international icon. The blue blur has remained relevant thanks to a steady stream of video games, TV shows, movies, comics, and more. There are few characters in gaming that have managed to remain as popular over the years, and Sonic helped make Sega a household name.

2026 marks the 35th anniversary of Sonic the Hedgehog , and Sega is celebrating the occasion in a number of different ways. New DLC was revealed for Sonic Racing: CrossWorlds at Summer Game Fest, which will add content based on Godzilla and Evangelion . Sega also pulled back the curtain on a new spin-off game called Sonic Pico Park .

Synthetic DNA in a Sonic Statue

New video game content is unsurprising for the anniversary of a major gaming icon, but Sega's collaboration with LOM BABY is certainly unique. Using biotechnology, LOM BABY has created an artificial DNA strand made up of all the core elements of Sonic the Hedgehog . These core elements include Sonic's spin dash, his heroism, and even his struggles with water. That strand was then placed inside a green Chaos Emerald within the transparent chest of the statue. LOM BABY has done similar art collaborations with gaming companies, including a recreation of the Resident Evil T-Virus earlier this year.

Who’s That Character?

Identify the silhouettes before time runs out.

0

—

5.0 s
0 / 10
Results
0
High Score: 0 —
More Games

It's unclear whether Sega will bring the LOM BABY collaboration to other countries, or if this will remain exclusive to Japan. The statue can be found in Sega's Tokyo Store location starting on June 23. That's just two days after the 35th anniversary of the original Sonic the Hedgehog game's release on the Sega Genesis, so the timing lines up fairly well. The whole thing is pretty unusual, but it should make for an interesting display piece for Sonic fans that happen to be in the area.

While 2026 marks a significant milestone for the character, it seems like next year could be even bigger for the franchise. Sonic the Hedgehog 4 comes to theaters in 2027, continuing Paramount's highly-successful film franchise. The movie will seemingly put a big focus on Amy Rose and Metal Sonic, two characters that made their big-screen debut at the end of Sonic the Hedgehog 3 . The movies have helped introduce a lot of new fans to the franchise, and Sega has taken advantage by releasing new games to coincide. That's exactly what happened in 2024, when Sonic x Shadow Generations was released on multiple platforms around the same time Sonic the Hedgehog 3 was in theaters.

Find the odd one out before the timer hits zero.

Round 1

Which one doesn't belong?

More Games

Best: 0/5 —
More Games

Sega's plans for future games are a mystery at the moment, but a Sonic CD remake has been rumored. Amy and Metal Sonic made their debuts in that game, so the timing could line up with the theatrical arrival of Sonic the Hedgehog 4 . Sonic Racing: CrossWorlds will also continue to see support in 2027, and the developers have indicated that there might be even more characters and tracks added if there's enough demand from players. The Sonic series might be turning 35 this year, but it doesn't seem to be slowing down as it gets older.

Source: Sega PR

item.title: How to Get and Use Algae in Solarpunk
item.description:
The more advanced your technology gets in Solarpunk , the easier farming and flying become. However, more materials will soon be needed for highly efficient machines and upgrades, and one material you'll need is Algae . Below, we'll share where you can find Algae and what purpose it serves!

How to Get Algae in Solarpunk

Algae can be found in natural spawns in the wild, just like plants and ore. However, more of this item cannot be generated right away. Instead, you'll need to progress the game to a certain point, which we'll explain below.

Upgrade Your Airship

To reach the Algae location, you'll need to have the Level 4 Airship Upgrade so you can travel to further away islands . To get Airship upgrades, you'll need to give items to Tradebot at the island with the blimps around it until you reach the trade for the required upgrades.

Explore New Islands

When you get your Airship to Level 4, there's an island Southeast of your starting island in the shape of a V . There is a stream that flows across this island and into a large body of water in the middle. Along the streams and mostly in the larger body of water, you will see green floating leaves that kind of look like seaweed. These are Algae and can be picked up just by walking up to them and interacting with them.

Once you begin automating things and really advancing the technology on your farm, you'll have the option to place an Autofisher . These will not only collect fish from bodies of water, but also Algae that pop up.

How to Use Algae in Solarpunk

You will need x1 Algae to unlock Tier 4 of research at the Energy Bench in Tradebot's shop . Once you have Tier 4 unlocked, this will also open up the ability to use a Blueprint to research the crafting of an Algae Drone .

The Algae Drone will automatically get Algae from the lakes and bring it back to the base , similar to Transport Drones . You can craft it at the Energy Workbench .

Unlocking Tier 4 at the Energy Bench also gives you the Circuitboard Recipe, which is a crafting component for later machines. To make one, you need:

  • x10 Algae
  • x5 Copper
  • x5 Cobalt

After this point, Circuitboards are used to make a lot of your automated machinery that makes your farm a lot more hands-off.

Solarpunk

Follow
Followed
Open-World
Survival
Base Building
Systems
Released
June 8, 2026
Developer(s)
Cyberwave
Publisher(s)
rokaplay, Metaroot
Multiplayer
Online Co-Op
Number of Players
Single-player
4 Images

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

Genre(s)
Open-World, Survival, Base Building
Powered by
item.content:encoded:
The more advanced your technology gets in Solarpunk , the easier farming and flying become. However, more materials will soon be needed for highly efficient machines and upgrades, and one material you'll need is Algae . Below, we'll share where you can find Algae and what purpose it serves!

How to Get Algae in Solarpunk

Algae can be found in natural spawns in the wild, just like plants and ore. However, more of this item cannot be generated right away. Instead, you'll need to progress the game to a certain point, which we'll explain below.

Upgrade Your Airship

To reach the Algae location, you'll need to have the Level 4 Airship Upgrade so you can travel to further away islands . To get Airship upgrades, you'll need to give items to Tradebot at the island with the blimps around it until you reach the trade for the required upgrades.

Explore New Islands

When you get your Airship to Level 4, there's an island Southeast of your starting island in the shape of a V . There is a stream that flows across this island and into a large body of water in the middle. Along the streams and mostly in the larger body of water, you will see green floating leaves that kind of look like seaweed. These are Algae and can be picked up just by walking up to them and interacting with them.

Once you begin automating things and really advancing the technology on your farm, you'll have the option to place an Autofisher . These will not only collect fish from bodies of water, but also Algae that pop up.

How to Use Algae in Solarpunk

You will need x1 Algae to unlock Tier 4 of research at the Energy Bench in Tradebot's shop . Once you have Tier 4 unlocked, this will also open up the ability to use a Blueprint to research the crafting of an Algae Drone .

The Algae Drone will automatically get Algae from the lakes and bring it back to the base , similar to Transport Drones . You can craft it at the Energy Workbench .

Unlocking Tier 4 at the Energy Bench also gives you the Circuitboard Recipe, which is a crafting component for later machines. To make one, you need:

  • x10 Algae
  • x5 Copper
  • x5 Cobalt

After this point, Circuitboards are used to make a lot of your automated machinery that makes your farm a lot more hands-off.

Solarpunk

item.title: 15 Best PS Plus Extra Games You Should Be Playing Right Now (June 2026)
item.description:
June 16 Update Notes (by Mark Sammut): I gave the article a cleansing, streamlining the selection down to the best 15 games on PS Plus. I also added Final Fantasy 16, June 2026's biggest new PS Plus game, while Risk of Rain 2 is the "final call" pick.

On June 13, 2022, Sony launched a new and improved PlayStation Plus in North America. Split into three tiers, this model combines the previous version of PS Plus with PS Now; depending on which level someone subscribes to, they will gain access to certain services and games.

  • PlayStation Plus Essential ($9.99/month): This tier is the equivalent of the old PS Plus. A subscription includes online access, monthly free games, and discounts.
  • PlayStation Plus Extra ($14.99/month): Along with the Essential tier benefits, Extra provides access to hundreds of PS4 and PS5 games.
  • PlayStation Plus Premium ($17.99/month): Along with the Essential and Extra tier benefits, Premium includes a library of classic games (PS3, PS2, PSP, and PS1), trials, and cloud streaming in certain regions.

PS Plus has over 700 games covering more than two decades of PlayStation history. Such a huge collection can be overwhelming, and the PS Plus app does not make it very easy to browse through the library; as such, it can be useful to know this tier's highlights before investing in a subscription. Each month, Sony adds a handful of new games, most of which are PS5 releases. Let's take a look at some of the best PS Plus Extra games .

15 Final Fantasy 16 (New PS Plus Game – June 2026)

The Biggest New PS Plus Game Of 2026

Final Fantasy 16
10 /10
Released
June 22, 2023

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL
PHYSICAL

Developer(s)
Square Enix
Genre(s)
Action RPG
Platform(s)
PC, PS5
Powered by

With Kingdom Come Deliverance , Life is Strange: Double Exposure , and Sonic X Shadow Generations , June 2026's PS Plus lineup delivers quality across a wide range of genres. As big as all three of those games are, Final Fantasy 16 comfortably towers over them as the month's standout release, along with being among the best new PS Plus games of 2026.

Now, it should be said that FF16 moves away from the franchise's traditional JRPG structure, playing out like an action game with light RPG elements. Rather than controlling a party in turn-based combat or swapping between multiple fighters in real-time battles, FF16 features a solitary playable character, Clive. While companions join him, they act on their own and cannot be issued commands or anything of the sort. These changes might not be exactly what every fan wants from Square Enix's massive series, but I would still advise giving FF16 a try to see if its dark medieval story, epic large-scale Eikon battles, and fast-paced combat hit the mark.

Full disclosure, Devil May Cry is my favorite franchise of all time, so I was always predisposed to falling in love with FF16 . However, I found the hybrid combat in the FF7 trilogy to be more engaging, and I probably should not have gone into FF16 hoping for something similar.

Go in with your mind open and free of expectations.

14 Final Call: Risk of Rain 2

Leaving PS Plus Extra In July 2026

Risk of Rain 2
Released
September 11, 2020

WHERE TO PLAY

SUBSCRIPTION
DIGITAL
PHYSICAL

Developer(s)
Hopoo Games
Genre(s)
Third-Person Shooter, Roguelike
Platform(s)
Xbox One, Nintendo Switch, PC, PS4
Powered by

Nothing lasts forever, and that extends to PS Plus games. Every month, a few titles depart Sony's service, and they hurt. In June, Red Dead Redemption and We Love Katamari Reroll left PS Plus, and they were hardly the only departures. If there's something you have been putting off playing, don't believe it will always be around, as a time will come when that's no longer the case.

Fortunately, Sony tends to give a month's notice for departures, providing enough time for subscribers to complete one or two titles. The July exit squad includes Get Even , Tropico 6 , Source of Madness , and Roki , along with a few more names. The upcoming month's biggest retirement will be Risk of Rain 2 , arguably one of the best games on PS Plus Extra. Honestly, I'm almost hesitant to recommend newcomers to start now, as I know full well that a month is nowhere near enough time to get your fill of Hopoo Games' masterpiece. However, RoR2 goes on sale fairly frequently, so it might be worth trying it out to see if a purchase could be required in the future.

13 Red Dead Redemption 2

Rockstar's Open-World Masterpiece Is On PS Plus

Red Dead Redemption 2
10 /10
Released
October 26, 2018

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL
PHYSICAL
FX Wiki Wiki

Developer(s)
Rockstar Games
Genre(s)
Action, Adventure
Platform(s)
PS4, Xbox One, PC, Stadia
Powered by

Well, you cannot get much better than arguably the greatest open-world game of all time, or at least a top contender. Now, Red Dead Redemption 2 is a massive deal regardless of the circumstances, but it should be noted that Rockstar's masterpiece was already part of PS Plus Extra, but it left the service in November 2024. That is about 18 months ago, so it is cool to have the game pack. Considering rumors of a GTA 6 reveal have been making the rounds, this arrival would give you something to play while waiting for that game to release. Or, it could be a complete coincidence.

What is there left to be said about RDR2 ? If you have never stepped into this universe, I heavily recommend playing the original Red Dead Redemption , which is also on PS Plus Extra, before jumping into the second entry. Yes, RDR2 is a prequel, but that is irrelevant. Otherwise, this game is for anybody looking for an immersive Western that tells a best-in-gaming story featuring characters you will come to love (or hate). The controls and gameplay can take some getting used to, though.

GTA 5 are also on PS Plus Extra.

12 Marvel's Spider-Man 2

The Most Super Game On PS Plus Extra

Marvel's Spider-Man 2
9 /10
Released
October 20, 2023

WHERE TO PLAY

SUBSCRIPTION
DIGITAL
PHYSICAL

Developer(s)
Insomniac Games
Genre(s)
Open-World, Action-Adventure, Superhero
Platform(s)
PlayStation 5, PC
Powered by

Insomniac's full Spider-Man trilogy can be played with a PS Plus Extra subscription, and they combine to provide more than 50 hours of superhero greatness. When it comes to picking the best of the bunch, a strong argument could be made for all three of them; however, there is absolutely no point in doing that when we can just recommend all three games.

Spider-Man has received his fair share of great games over the decades, but Marvel's Spider-Man and its sequels are the most complete packages, delivering fantastic web-swinging, satisfying combat, quality visuals, and mostly decent stories. All three games are set in New York, and they recreate the city pretty darn well. While none of the open-worlds have amazing optional content by the standards of the genre, moving through them is so much fun that they are still a blast.

Marvel's Spider-Man 2 has the most divisive story in the trilogy, but it also fine-tunes the gameplay to near perfection. You also get to play as Miles and Peter, both of whom have nuances that set them apart from each other.

11 Like A Dragon: Infinite Wealth

Maybe The Best Turn-Based RPG On PS Plus

Like A Dragon: Infinite Wealth
9 /10
Released
January 26, 2024

WHERE TO PLAY

SUBSCRIPTION
DIGITAL
PHYSICAL

Developer(s)
Ryu Ga Gotoku Studio
Genre(s)
RPG
Platform(s)
PC, PlayStation 5, PlayStation 4, Xbox One, Xbox Series X
Powered by

I am almost hesitant to recommend Like A Dragon: Infinite Wealth . Why? Because it will be the only game you play for a month or maybe even two, and that is only if you average at least 15 hours of gaming per week. Ryu Ga Gotoku Studio's franchise is known for its lengthy campaigns that combine an incredible main story with an absurd range of optional quests, but Infinite Wealth took things a few steps further. This is, by far, the longest entry in the franchise, one that will dominate your life and become your everything for a while.

Besides quantity, Infinite Wealth also delivers quality. Set in Hawaii and splitting its time between two protagonists, the story builds on Like a Dragon and The Man Who Erased His Name , emphasizing both Kazuma and Ichiban. The latter is the primary focus, but the former gets his moments in the spotlight as well. Honolulu is gorgeous, huge (by Yakuza standards), and densely populated with content, including sub-stories that are regularly incredible. The turn-based combat isn't that unusual for the genre, but it is all-around great.

10 The Last Of Us Part 1

Possibly The Best Game On PS Plus

The Last of Us Part I
9 /10
Released
September 2, 2022

WHERE TO PLAY

SUBSCRIPTION
DIGITAL
PHYSICAL

Developer(s)
Naughty Dog
PS Plus Availability
Extra & Premium
Platform(s)
PC, PlayStation 5
Genre(s)
Action-Adventure
Powered by

Naughty Dog's The Last of Us brought the PS3 era to an end on the strongest possible note, producing an industry-defining game that has gone on to produce a big sequel and a highly-regarded TV adaptation. While the original release is not on PS Plus Premium, subscribers can check out the PS4 remaster, which is fantastic. PS5 owners can also play The Last of Us Part 1 , a 2022 remake that gives the classic release a pretty thorough makeover. Honestly, if someone has already played through the original or remastered versions, they might not get too much out of the remake since the best part, the story, is unchanged. However, newcomers can just jump into this iteration.

The Last of Us follows Joel and Ellie as they go on a harrowing journey through a post-apocalyptic United States, all in the hopes of finding a group that might be able to create a cure using the latter's immunity to a fungus that has eradicated most humanity. Blending stealth, melee combat, and exploration, the gameplay sections are intense, visceral, and relatively challenging. More importantly, the story and characters are phenomenal, comfortably among the best in PlayStation history.

9 Bloodborne

No, This Is Probably The Best Game On PS Plus

Bloodborne
Released
March 24, 2015

WHERE TO PLAY

SUBSCRIPTION
DIGITAL
FX Wiki Wiki

Developer(s)
From Software
PS Plus Availability
Extra & Premium
Platform(s)
PlayStation 4
Genre(s)
Action, RPG, Soulslike
6 Images
Powered by

Boasting a Gothic aesthetic and reflex-based combat that demands aggression from players, Bloodborne delivers a kinetic experience that shines on a visual, auditory, and gameplay level. Naturally, FromSoftware's masterpiece is not an easy game, but it is one of the most rewarding PS4 exclusives. Consequently, it is easily among the best games on PS Plus , while also being a personal favorite of mine.

Although not devoid of technical and performance issues, Bloodborne 's environments, combat, boss designs, atmosphere, and storytelling are arguably among the very best in the Soulslike genre, if not just action RPGs in general. Therefore, I recommend pushing through the first hour or so until you start to get used to the lower framerate compared to more modern releases.

Both versions of Demon's Souls are also on PS Plus.

8 Cyberpunk 2077

The Long Road To Redemption Leads To One Of The Greatest Open-World Games Ever

Cyberpunk 2077
6 /10
Released
December 10, 2020

WHERE TO PLAY

SUBSCRIPTION
DIGITAL
PHYSICAL
FX Wiki Wiki GGMods Mods

Developer(s)
CD Projekt Red
PS Plus Availability
Extra & Premium
Genre(s)
RPG, Action
Platform(s)
PlayStation 5, PlayStation 4, Xbox Series X, Xbox Series S, Xbox One, Nintendo Switch 2, PC
6 Images
Powered by

In 2020, Cyberpunk 2077 was the most controversial game on the market, particularly the PS4-era version. The PS5 iteration was always playable, but certain issues kept it from being an ideal experience. Jump forward a few years, and CD Projekt Red's sci-fi RPG has made a near-full recovery, not only establishing itself as the ultimate comeback story but also proving successful enough that even iterative updates like the upcoming 2.3 are capable of sparking widespread excitement.

Set in the impressive Night City, Cyberpunk 2077 blends clever character writing with an ambitious overarching storyline and enjoyable first-person gunplay that is backed by augmentation options. The game is not very difficult by any stretch of the imagination, but V can be turned into a walking god that destroys everyone, which is fun in its own right. If this is your first time trying Cyberpunk 2077 , you are in store for what should be a fantastic experience.

7 Horizon Zero Dawn Remastered

Well, Aloy's First Adventure Is Back On PS Plus Extra

Horizon Zero Dawn Remastered
8 /10
Released
October 31, 2024

WHERE TO PLAY

SUBSCRIPTION
DIGITAL

Developer(s)
Guerrilla Games, Nixxes
Genre(s)
Action RPG, Adventure
Platform(s)
PC, PlayStation 5
Powered by

This is a bit of a strange one. Of course, Horizon Zero Dawn Remastered is the biggest April 2026 new PS Plus game, and by quite a margin. After all, we are talking about a massive console exclusive that launched a franchise that is still going strong and will hopefully produce many more great sagas. Frankly, HZD should be on PS Plus in some form or another, and it was frustrating (but understandable) when the non-remastered version was pulled from the service in 2024, just a couple of months before its improved iteration became available.

Still, subscribers had Horizon Zero Dawn for years before it was taken away, and that included the complete edition with "The Frozen Wilds" DLC. Because of this history, it is a bit difficult to get too excited about this new arrival, just because we are essentially getting a game we already had at one point.

That out of the way, Horizon Zero Dawn Remastered is one of the best open-world games on PS Plus, and the genre isn't really necessary. If you have only played Forbidden West , I would definitely recommend going back and playing its predecessor, especially since the remaster adopts quite a few elements from the sequel. HZD looks gorgeous, plays well, and tells a slow-burning but great storyline.

6 God Of War Ragnarok

One Of The Best PlayStation Console Exclusives On PS Plus

God of War Ragnarok
10 /10
Released
November 9, 2022

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL
PHYSICAL

Developer(s)
Santa Monica Studio
PS Plus Availability
Extra & Premium
Platform(s)
PC, PlayStation 4, PlayStation 5
Genre(s)
Action, Adventure
Powered by

A direct sequel to 2018's God of War , Ragnarok brings the franchise's Norse saga to an end, or at least I assume that is the case. As suggested by the title, the story revolves around the pending onset of Ragnarok, an end-of-the-world event said to coincide with the destruction of the realms and the gods. Kratos wants to stop that, but he is also on the cusp of war with Odin; in the meantime, the All-Father is trying to bring Atreus to his side.

Gameplay-wise, Ragnarok plays similarly to 2018's God of War , albeit with a few tweaks and the introduction of a new weapon. The combat is still crunchy, satisfying, and intense; that said, if someone hated the system in the previous game, they likely will not get too much out of the sequel. While Atreus is still the main companion, Kratos is also aided by a few other characters, all of whom have unique abilities that are fun to use in battles. The campaign is fairly long and generally ambitious, blending character-driven moments with an epic sense of scale. Ragnarok also comes with Valhalla, an epilogue mode that is just all-around brilliant.
item.content:encoded:
June 16 Update Notes (by Mark Sammut): I gave the article a cleansing, streamlining the selection down to the best 15 games on PS Plus. I also added Final Fantasy 16, June 2026's biggest new PS Plus game, while Risk of Rain 2 is the "final call" pick.

On June 13, 2022, Sony launched a new and improved PlayStation Plus in North America. Split into three tiers, this model combines the previous version of PS Plus with PS Now; depending on which level someone subscribes to, they will gain access to certain services and games.

  • PlayStation Plus Essential ($9.99/month): This tier is the equivalent of the old PS Plus. A subscription includes online access, monthly free games, and discounts.
  • PlayStation Plus Extra ($14.99/month): Along with the Essential tier benefits, Extra provides access to hundreds of PS4 and PS5 games.
  • PlayStation Plus Premium ($17.99/month): Along with the Essential and Extra tier benefits, Premium includes a library of classic games (PS3, PS2, PSP, and PS1), trials, and cloud streaming in certain regions.

PS Plus has over 700 games covering more than two decades of PlayStation history. Such a huge collection can be overwhelming, and the PS Plus app does not make it very easy to browse through the library; as such, it can be useful to know this tier's highlights before investing in a subscription. Each month, Sony adds a handful of new games, most of which are PS5 releases. Let's take a look at some of the best PS Plus Extra games .

15 Final Fantasy 16 (New PS Plus Game – June 2026)

The Biggest New PS Plus Game Of 2026

Final Fantasy 16
10 /10
Released
June 22, 2023

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL
PHYSICAL

Developer(s)
Square Enix
Genre(s)
Action RPG
Platform(s)
PC, PS5
Powered by

With Kingdom Come Deliverance , Life is Strange: Double Exposure , and Sonic X Shadow Generations , June 2026's PS Plus lineup delivers quality across a wide range of genres. As big as all three of those games are, Final Fantasy 16 comfortably towers over them as the month's standout release, along with being among the best new PS Plus games of 2026.

Now, it should be said that FF16 moves away from the franchise's traditional JRPG structure, playing out like an action game with light RPG elements. Rather than controlling a party in turn-based combat or swapping between multiple fighters in real-time battles, FF16 features a solitary playable character, Clive. While companions join him, they act on their own and cannot be issued commands or anything of the sort. These changes might not be exactly what every fan wants from Square Enix's massive series, but I would still advise giving FF16 a try to see if its dark medieval story, epic large-scale Eikon battles, and fast-paced combat hit the mark.

Full disclosure, Devil May Cry is my favorite franchise of all time, so I was always predisposed to falling in love with FF16 . However, I found the hybrid combat in the FF7 trilogy to be more engaging, and I probably should not have gone into FF16 hoping for something similar.

Go in with your mind open and free of expectations.

14 Final Call: Risk of Rain 2

Leaving PS Plus Extra In July 2026

Risk of Rain 2
Released
September 11, 2020

WHERE TO PLAY

SUBSCRIPTION
DIGITAL
PHYSICAL

Developer(s)
Hopoo Games
Genre(s)
Third-Person Shooter, Roguelike
Platform(s)
Xbox One, Nintendo Switch, PC, PS4
Powered by

Nothing lasts forever, and that extends to PS Plus games. Every month, a few titles depart Sony's service, and they hurt. In June, Red Dead Redemption and We Love Katamari Reroll left PS Plus, and they were hardly the only departures. If there's something you have been putting off playing, don't believe it will always be around, as a time will come when that's no longer the case.

Fortunately, Sony tends to give a month's notice for departures, providing enough time for subscribers to complete one or two titles. The July exit squad includes Get Even , Tropico 6 , Source of Madness , and Roki , along with a few more names. The upcoming month's biggest retirement will be Risk of Rain 2 , arguably one of the best games on PS Plus Extra. Honestly, I'm almost hesitant to recommend newcomers to start now, as I know full well that a month is nowhere near enough time to get your fill of Hopoo Games' masterpiece. However, RoR2 goes on sale fairly frequently, so it might be worth trying it out to see if a purchase could be required in the future.

13 Red Dead Redemption 2

Rockstar's Open-World Masterpiece Is On PS Plus

Red Dead Redemption 2
10 /10
Released
October 26, 2018

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL
PHYSICAL
FX Wiki Wiki

Developer(s)
Rockstar Games
Genre(s)
Action, Adventure
Platform(s)
PS4, Xbox One, PC, Stadia
Powered by

Well, you cannot get much better than arguably the greatest open-world game of all time, or at least a top contender. Now, Red Dead Redemption 2 is a massive deal regardless of the circumstances, but it should be noted that Rockstar's masterpiece was already part of PS Plus Extra, but it left the service in November 2024. That is about 18 months ago, so it is cool to have the game pack. Considering rumors of a GTA 6 reveal have been making the rounds, this arrival would give you something to play while waiting for that game to release. Or, it could be a complete coincidence.

What is there left to be said about RDR2 ? If you have never stepped into this universe, I heavily recommend playing the original Red Dead Redemption , which is also on PS Plus Extra, before jumping into the second entry. Yes, RDR2 is a prequel, but that is irrelevant. Otherwise, this game is for anybody looking for an immersive Western that tells a best-in-gaming story featuring characters you will come to love (or hate). The controls and gameplay can take some getting used to, though.

GTA 5 are also on PS Plus Extra.

12 Marvel's Spider-Man 2

The Most Super Game On PS Plus Extra

Marvel's Spider-Man 2
9 /10
Released
October 20, 2023

WHERE TO PLAY

SUBSCRIPTION
DIGITAL
PHYSICAL

Developer(s)
Insomniac Games
Genre(s)
Open-World, Action-Adventure, Superhero
Platform(s)
PlayStation 5, PC
Powered by

Insomniac's full Spider-Man trilogy can be played with a PS Plus Extra subscription, and they combine to provide more than 50 hours of superhero greatness. When it comes to picking the best of the bunch, a strong argument could be made for all three of them; however, there is absolutely no point in doing that when we can just recommend all three games.

Spider-Man has received his fair share of great games over the decades, but Marvel's Spider-Man and its sequels are the most complete packages, delivering fantastic web-swinging, satisfying combat, quality visuals, and mostly decent stories. All three games are set in New York, and they recreate the city pretty darn well. While none of the open-worlds have amazing optional content by the standards of the genre, moving through them is so much fun that they are still a blast.

Marvel's Spider-Man 2 has the most divisive story in the trilogy, but it also fine-tunes the gameplay to near perfection. You also get to play as Miles and Peter, both of whom have nuances that set them apart from each other.

11 Like A Dragon: Infinite Wealth

Maybe The Best Turn-Based RPG On PS Plus

Like A Dragon: Infinite Wealth
9 /10
Released
January 26, 2024

WHERE TO PLAY

SUBSCRIPTION
DIGITAL
PHYSICAL

Developer(s)
Ryu Ga Gotoku Studio
Genre(s)
RPG
Platform(s)
PC, PlayStation 5, PlayStation 4, Xbox One, Xbox Series X
Powered by

I am almost hesitant to recommend Like A Dragon: Infinite Wealth . Why? Because it will be the only game you play for a month or maybe even two, and that is only if you average at least 15 hours of gaming per week. Ryu Ga Gotoku Studio's franchise is known for its lengthy campaigns that combine an incredible main story with an absurd range of optional quests, but Infinite Wealth took things a few steps further. This is, by far, the longest entry in the franchise, one that will dominate your life and become your everything for a while.

Besides quantity, Infinite Wealth also delivers quality. Set in Hawaii and splitting its time between two protagonists, the story builds on Like a Dragon and The Man Who Erased His Name , emphasizing both Kazuma and Ichiban. The latter is the primary focus, but the former gets his moments in the spotlight as well. Honolulu is gorgeous, huge (by Yakuza standards), and densely populated with content, including sub-stories that are regularly incredible. The turn-based combat isn't that unusual for the genre, but it is all-around great.

10 The Last Of Us Part 1

Possibly The Best Game On PS Plus

The Last of Us Part I
9 /10
Released
September 2, 2022

WHERE TO PLAY

SUBSCRIPTION
DIGITAL
PHYSICAL

Developer(s)
Naughty Dog
PS Plus Availability
Extra & Premium
Platform(s)
PC, PlayStation 5
Genre(s)
Action-Adventure
Powered by

Naughty Dog's The Last of Us brought the PS3 era to an end on the strongest possible note, producing an industry-defining game that has gone on to produce a big sequel and a highly-regarded TV adaptation. While the original release is not on PS Plus Premium, subscribers can check out the PS4 remaster, which is fantastic. PS5 owners can also play The Last of Us Part 1 , a 2022 remake that gives the classic release a pretty thorough makeover. Honestly, if someone has already played through the original or remastered versions, they might not get too much out of the remake since the best part, the story, is unchanged. However, newcomers can just jump into this iteration.

The Last of Us follows Joel and Ellie as they go on a harrowing journey through a post-apocalyptic United States, all in the hopes of finding a group that might be able to create a cure using the latter's immunity to a fungus that has eradicated most humanity. Blending stealth, melee combat, and exploration, the gameplay sections are intense, visceral, and relatively challenging. More importantly, the story and characters are phenomenal, comfortably among the best in PlayStation history.

9 Bloodborne

No, This Is Probably The Best Game On PS Plus

Bloodborne
Released
March 24, 2015

WHERE TO PLAY

SUBSCRIPTION
DIGITAL
FX Wiki Wiki

Developer(s)
From Software
PS Plus Availability
Extra & Premium
Platform(s)
PlayStation 4
Genre(s)
Action, RPG, Soulslike
6 Images
Powered by

Boasting a Gothic aesthetic and reflex-based combat that demands aggression from players, Bloodborne delivers a kinetic experience that shines on a visual, auditory, and gameplay level. Naturally, FromSoftware's masterpiece is not an easy game, but it is one of the most rewarding PS4 exclusives. Consequently, it is easily among the best games on PS Plus , while also being a personal favorite of mine.

Although not devoid of technical and performance issues, Bloodborne 's environments, combat, boss designs, atmosphere, and storytelling are arguably among the very best in the Soulslike genre, if not just action RPGs in general. Therefore, I recommend pushing through the first hour or so until you start to get used to the lower framerate compared to more modern releases.

Both versions of Demon's Souls are also on PS Plus.

8 Cyberpunk 2077

The Long Road To Redemption Leads To One Of The Greatest Open-World Games Ever

Cyberpunk 2077
6 /10
Released
December 10, 2020

WHERE TO PLAY

SUBSCRIPTION
DIGITAL
PHYSICAL
FX Wiki Wiki GGMods Mods

Developer(s)
CD Projekt Red
PS Plus Availability
Extra & Premium
Genre(s)
RPG, Action
Platform(s)
PlayStation 5, PlayStation 4, Xbox Series X, Xbox Series S, Xbox One, Nintendo Switch 2, PC
6 Images
Powered by

In 2020, Cyberpunk 2077 was the most controversial game on the market, particularly the PS4-era version. The PS5 iteration was always playable, but certain issues kept it from being an ideal experience. Jump forward a few years, and CD Projekt Red's sci-fi RPG has made a near-full recovery, not only establishing itself as the ultimate comeback story but also proving successful enough that even iterative updates like the upcoming 2.3 are capable of sparking widespread excitement.

Set in the impressive Night City, Cyberpunk 2077 blends clever character writing with an ambitious overarching storyline and enjoyable first-person gunplay that is backed by augmentation options. The game is not very difficult by any stretch of the imagination, but V can be turned into a walking god that destroys everyone, which is fun in its own right. If this is your first time trying Cyberpunk 2077 , you are in store for what should be a fantastic experience.

7 Horizon Zero Dawn Remastered

Well, Aloy's First Adventure Is Back On PS Plus Extra

Horizon Zero Dawn Remastered
8 /10
Released
October 31, 2024

WHERE TO PLAY

SUBSCRIPTION
DIGITAL

Developer(s)
Guerrilla Games, Nixxes
Genre(s)
Action RPG, Adventure
Platform(s)
PC, PlayStation 5
Powered by

This is a bit of a strange one. Of course, Horizon Zero Dawn Remastered is the biggest April 2026 new PS Plus game, and by quite a margin. After all, we are talking about a massive console exclusive that launched a franchise that is still going strong and will hopefully produce many more great sagas. Frankly, HZD should be on PS Plus in some form or another, and it was frustrating (but understandable) when the non-remastered version was pulled from the service in 2024, just a couple of months before its improved iteration became available.

Still, subscribers had Horizon Zero Dawn for years before it was taken away, and that included the complete edition with "The Frozen Wilds" DLC. Because of this history, it is a bit difficult to get too excited about this new arrival, just because we are essentially getting a game we already had at one point.

That out of the way, Horizon Zero Dawn Remastered is one of the best open-world games on PS Plus, and the genre isn't really necessary. If you have only played Forbidden West , I would definitely recommend going back and playing its predecessor, especially since the remaster adopts quite a few elements from the sequel. HZD looks gorgeous, plays well, and tells a slow-burning but great storyline.

6 God Of War Ragnarok

One Of The Best PlayStation Console Exclusives On PS Plus

God of War Ragnarok
10 /10
Released
November 9, 2022

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL
PHYSICAL

Developer(s)
Santa Monica Studio
PS Plus Availability
Extra & Premium
Platform(s)
PC, PlayStation 4, PlayStation 5
Genre(s)
Action, Adventure
Powered by

A direct sequel to 2018's God of War , Ragnarok brings the franchise's Norse saga to an end, or at least I assume that is the case. As suggested by the title, the story revolves around the pending onset of Ragnarok, an end-of-the-world event said to coincide with the destruction of the realms and the gods. Kratos wants to stop that, but he is also on the cusp of war with Odin; in the meantime, the All-Father is trying to bring Atreus to his side.

Gameplay-wise, Ragnarok plays similarly to 2018's God of War , albeit with a few tweaks and the introduction of a new weapon. The combat is still crunchy, satisfying, and intense; that said, if someone hated the system in the previous game, they likely will not get too much out of the sequel. While Atreus is still the main companion, Kratos is also aided by a few other characters, all of whom have unique abilities that are fun to use in battles. The campaign is fairly long and generally ambitious, blending character-driven moments with an epic sense of scale. Ragnarok also comes with Valhalla, an epilogue mode that is just all-around brilliant.

item.title: Shrek 5 Trailer Officially Brings the Ogre Back, But It Shouldn't Be His Only Comeback
item.description:
The Shrek 5 trailer has finally been revealed, with the movie now set to release in theaters on June 30, 2027. Mike Myers, Eddie Murphy, and Cameron Diaz are all back, giving DreamWorks' most famous ogre his first proper movie since Shrek Forever After in 2010. That means Shrek 5 is arriving 17 years after the last mainline Shrek film, which is wild for a franchise that still feels like such a big part of present pop culture.

If Shrek is officially making his movie comeback, though, then there's no reason his return should stop at theaters. The franchise has been away from gaming for almost as long, and that absence feels especially strange now that licensed games are starting to feel exciting again. Before Shrek 5 arrives, DreamWorks should seriously consider giving another chance to the strangest Shrek game of them all, Shrek SuperSlam .

Shrek SuperSlam Was One of the Franchise's Weirdest Gaming Experiments

Shrek SuperSlam was released in 2005, between Shrek 2 and Shrek the Third , and it didn't try to simply retell the plot from one of the movies. Instead, it turned the franchise into a chaotic fighting game where characters from the Shrek universe slammed each other through walls, windows, and fairy-tale arenas. Players could control characters like Shrek, Donkey, Puss in Boots, Fiona, Prince Charming, and other familiar faces in a game designed purely around over-the-top brawling—similarly to games like Super Smash Bros.

Who’s That Character?

Identify the silhouettes before time runs out.

5.0 s
0 / 10

Results

0

High Score: 0

—

More Games
Who’s That Character? Identify the silhouettes before time runs out.
Easy (7.5s)Medium (5.0s)Hard (2.5s)Permadeath (2.5s)
5.0 s
0 / 10
Results
0
High Score: 0 —
More Games

That concept alone makes Shrek SuperSlam stand out from the rest of the franchise's gaming history. Plenty of Shrek games were built as platformers, party games, racing games, or direct movie tie-ins, but SuperSlam understood that Shrek 's world was strange enough to support something even sillier. It essentially treated the franchise like a playground of oddball characters who wanted to destroy one another rather than a movie plot that had to be copied scene by scene.

The game's main idea was simple, but it fit Shrek better than it probably had any right to. Every character had a SuperSlam attack, and the environments were fully destructible during matches. That meant the appeal wasn't just winning a fight, but watching fairy-tale chaos unfold in a way that felt very on brand for Shrek 's personality.

Shrek SuperSlam wasn't a critical masterpiece, though, although it did perform better than many other licensed games from its era. Metacritic lists the Xbox version of Shrek SuperSlam at 71, the GameCube version at 70, the PC version at 69, and the PlayStation 2 version at 67, while the handheld versions landed a bit lower. For a 2005 licensed Shrek fighting game, that's not exactly a disaster.

Plenty of Shrek games were built as platformers, party games, racing games, or direct movie tie-ins, but SuperSlam understood that Shrek 's world was strange enough to support something even sillier.

What critics and players seemed to like most about the game was its premise. Shrek SuperSlam had an immediately understandable appeal, especially for younger gamers, because it let them throw familiar characters into absurd battles with destructible stages and exaggerated attacks. It wasn't trying to be a polished competitive fighting game because it didn't need to be one, and that actually worked in its favor.

The problems were pretty clear as well. Some reviewers found the game too easy, too short, repetitive, or shallow, and the handheld versions didn't carry the same appeal as the console versions. It had the shape of a great Shrek party-fighting game, but it didn't have the depth or longevity to become a true franchise pillar.

But that's exactly why it feels like the right kind of game to revisit now that Shrek 5 is on the way. The original Shrek SuperSlam had a strong enough idea to be remembered, but enough obvious limitations that a modern revival could improve almost everything about it. A new version could keep the absurdity while adding better combat, arenas, multiplayer support, online play, and a much larger roster of characters from Dreamworks' Shrek universe.

Shrek has also had a much longer gaming history than some fans may remember. In fact, Shrek was once everywhere in games, even if not every release was good or especially memorable. Now that the movies are finally waking back up, the gaming industry shouldn't act like that entire part of the franchise simply never happened.

The Biggest Shrek Video Games Ever Released

  • Shrek (2001)
  • Shrek: Fairy Tale Freakdown (2001)
  • Shrek: Swamp Kart Speedway (2002)
  • Shrek: Treasure Hunt (2002)
  • Shrek Super Party (2002)
  • Shrek: Hassle at the Castle (2002)
  • Shrek Extra Large (2002)
  • Shrek 2 (2004)
  • Shrek 2: Beg for Mercy (2004)
  • Shrek SuperSlam (2005)
  • Shrek Smash n' Crash Racing (2006)
  • Shrek the Third (2007)
  • Shrek n' Roll (2007)
  • Shrek: Ogres & Dronkeys (2007)
  • Shrek's Carnival Craze (2008)
  • Shrek Kart (2009)
  • Shrek Forever After (2010)
  • Shrek's Fairytale Kingdom (2012)
  • Shrek Sugar Fever (2017)
  • Shrek Swamp Tycoon (2024)

Shrek's Gaming Comeback Doesn't Need to Be a Simple Movie Tie-In

A direct Shrek 5 game could work, but only if it avoided feeling like an old-school obligation. The licensed tie-in era often produced rushed games that existed mostly because a movie was about to hit theaters, and Shrek had its fair share of that. To justify its own existence, then, a new Shrek game would need to feel like a real project with its own reason to exist.

Guess the games from the emojis.

Gamoji

Guess the game from the emojis.

Time Left: 00:00

Title

—

Clues

—

Results

0 POINTS
—
More Games

Answers

Title

—

—

Clues

—

—

Guess the games from the emojis.
Easy (120s)Medium (90s)Hard (60s)

Gamoji

Guess the game from the emojis.
Time Left: 00:00
Title
—
Clues
—
Results
0 POINTS
—
More Games
Answers
Title
—
—
Clues
—
—

That's where Shrek SuperSlam makes more sense than a straightforward adaptation. A modern SuperSlam revival could use the excitement around Shrek 5 without being trapped by the movie's plot. It could bring back classic characters, add new characters from the sequel, and fold in the wider Shrek and Puss in Boots universe.

A direct Shrek 5 game could work, but only if it avoided feeling like an old-school obligation.

The timing is also better than it might seem. Licensed games have become a lot more interesting in recent years, with Insomniac's Marvel's Spider-Man games showing how powerful a high-quality adaptation can be. Marvel's Wolverine is now giving Insomniac another major licensed character to tackle, and Warhorse Studios is working on an open-world Lord of the Rings RPG.

In other words, the industry isn't just throwing away the idea of licensed games anymore. Indiana Jones and the Great Circle also proved that a classic movie franchise can work in games when the project is built around what makes that property fun to inhabit. If studios are willing to take Spider-Man , Wolverine , Indiana Jones , and Lord of the Rings seriously, then Shrek shouldn't be limited to nostalgia memes and theater seats.

Of course, Shrek doesn't need the same kind of prestige-game treatment as those franchises either. The right Shrek game should probably be funnier, weirder, and more chaotic than most modern licensed games. That's why SuperSlam is such a good starting point, because it already knew Shrek was at its best when fairy-tale rules were being broken.

Even if DreamWorks never revives Shrek SuperSlam specifically, Shrek 5 should still make a new game easier to justify. The franchise has the characters, settings, humor, and nostalgia to support something more ambitious than a quick mobile release. A co-op adventure, party game, brawler, or open-area fairy-tale comedy game could all make sense if the studio behind it understood the tone.

The biggest point is that Shrek 's comeback should arguably feel bigger than one movie. Shrek 5 is already bringing the ogre back to the big screen after a long absence, and that alone will probably be enough to make longtime fans curious. But if DreamWorks wants Shrek 's return to feel like a true franchise revival, gaming deserves to be part of it, whatever that might look like.

Shrek 5

Animation
Comedy
Family
Fantasy
Release Date
June 30, 2027
Director
Conrad Vernon, Walt Dohrn, Brad Ableson
Writers
Michael McCullers, William Steig, Christopher Meledandri

Cast

See All
  • Mike Myers
    Shrek (voice)
  • Eddie Murphy
    Donkey (voice)
  • Cameron Diaz
    Princess Fiona (voice)
  • Antonio Banderas
    Puss In Boots (voice)

Producers
Andrew Adamson, Chris Meledandri, Gina Shay, Jeffrey Katzenberg
Franchise(s)
Shrek
Powered by
item.content:encoded:
The Shrek 5 trailer has finally been revealed, with the movie now set to release in theaters on June 30, 2027. Mike Myers, Eddie Murphy, and Cameron Diaz are all back, giving DreamWorks' most famous ogre his first proper movie since Shrek Forever After in 2010. That means Shrek 5 is arriving 17 years after the last mainline Shrek film, which is wild for a franchise that still feels like such a big part of present pop culture.

If Shrek is officially making his movie comeback, though, then there's no reason his return should stop at theaters. The franchise has been away from gaming for almost as long, and that absence feels especially strange now that licensed games are starting to feel exciting again. Before Shrek 5 arrives, DreamWorks should seriously consider giving another chance to the strangest Shrek game of them all, Shrek SuperSlam .

Shrek SuperSlam Was One of the Franchise's Weirdest Gaming Experiments

Shrek SuperSlam was released in 2005, between Shrek 2 and Shrek the Third , and it didn't try to simply retell the plot from one of the movies. Instead, it turned the franchise into a chaotic fighting game where characters from the Shrek universe slammed each other through walls, windows, and fairy-tale arenas. Players could control characters like Shrek, Donkey, Puss in Boots, Fiona, Prince Charming, and other familiar faces in a game designed purely around over-the-top brawling—similarly to games like Super Smash Bros.

Who’s That Character?

Identify the silhouettes before time runs out.

5.0 s
0 / 10

Results

0

High Score: 0

—

More Games
Who’s That Character? Identify the silhouettes before time runs out.
Easy (7.5s)Medium (5.0s)Hard (2.5s)Permadeath (2.5s)
5.0 s
0 / 10
Results
0
High Score: 0 —
More Games

That concept alone makes Shrek SuperSlam stand out from the rest of the franchise's gaming history. Plenty of Shrek games were built as platformers, party games, racing games, or direct movie tie-ins, but SuperSlam understood that Shrek 's world was strange enough to support something even sillier. It essentially treated the franchise like a playground of oddball characters who wanted to destroy one another rather than a movie plot that had to be copied scene by scene.

The game's main idea was simple, but it fit Shrek better than it probably had any right to. Every character had a SuperSlam attack, and the environments were fully destructible during matches. That meant the appeal wasn't just winning a fight, but watching fairy-tale chaos unfold in a way that felt very on brand for Shrek 's personality.

Shrek SuperSlam wasn't a critical masterpiece, though, although it did perform better than many other licensed games from its era. Metacritic lists the Xbox version of Shrek SuperSlam at 71, the GameCube version at 70, the PC version at 69, and the PlayStation 2 version at 67, while the handheld versions landed a bit lower. For a 2005 licensed Shrek fighting game, that's not exactly a disaster.

Plenty of Shrek games were built as platformers, party games, racing games, or direct movie tie-ins, but SuperSlam understood that Shrek 's world was strange enough to support something even sillier.

What critics and players seemed to like most about the game was its premise. Shrek SuperSlam had an immediately understandable appeal, especially for younger gamers, because it let them throw familiar characters into absurd battles with destructible stages and exaggerated attacks. It wasn't trying to be a polished competitive fighting game because it didn't need to be one, and that actually worked in its favor.

The problems were pretty clear as well. Some reviewers found the game too easy, too short, repetitive, or shallow, and the handheld versions didn't carry the same appeal as the console versions. It had the shape of a great Shrek party-fighting game, but it didn't have the depth or longevity to become a true franchise pillar.

But that's exactly why it feels like the right kind of game to revisit now that Shrek 5 is on the way. The original Shrek SuperSlam had a strong enough idea to be remembered, but enough obvious limitations that a modern revival could improve almost everything about it. A new version could keep the absurdity while adding better combat, arenas, multiplayer support, online play, and a much larger roster of characters from Dreamworks' Shrek universe.

Shrek has also had a much longer gaming history than some fans may remember. In fact, Shrek was once everywhere in games, even if not every release was good or especially memorable. Now that the movies are finally waking back up, the gaming industry shouldn't act like that entire part of the franchise simply never happened.

The Biggest Shrek Video Games Ever Released

  • Shrek (2001)
  • Shrek: Fairy Tale Freakdown (2001)
  • Shrek: Swamp Kart Speedway (2002)
  • Shrek: Treasure Hunt (2002)
  • Shrek Super Party (2002)
  • Shrek: Hassle at the Castle (2002)
  • Shrek Extra Large (2002)
  • Shrek 2 (2004)
  • Shrek 2: Beg for Mercy (2004)
  • Shrek SuperSlam (2005)
  • Shrek Smash n' Crash Racing (2006)
  • Shrek the Third (2007)
  • Shrek n' Roll (2007)
  • Shrek: Ogres & Dronkeys (2007)
  • Shrek's Carnival Craze (2008)
  • Shrek Kart (2009)
  • Shrek Forever After (2010)
  • Shrek's Fairytale Kingdom (2012)
  • Shrek Sugar Fever (2017)
  • Shrek Swamp Tycoon (2024)

Shrek's Gaming Comeback Doesn't Need to Be a Simple Movie Tie-In

A direct Shrek 5 game could work, but only if it avoided feeling like an old-school obligation. The licensed tie-in era often produced rushed games that existed mostly because a movie was about to hit theaters, and Shrek had its fair share of that. To justify its own existence, then, a new Shrek game would need to feel like a real project with its own reason to exist.

Guess the games from the emojis.

Gamoji

Guess the game from the emojis.

Title

—

Clues

—

0 POINTS
—
More Games

—

—

—

—

Gamoji

Clues

—

That's where Shrek SuperSlam makes more sense than a straightforward adaptation. A modern SuperSlam revival could use the excitement around Shrek 5 without being trapped by the movie's plot. It could bring back classic characters, add new characters from the sequel, and fold in the wider Shrek and Puss in Boots universe.

A direct Shrek 5 game could work, but only if it avoided feeling like an old-school obligation.

The timing is also better than it might seem. Licensed games have become a lot more interesting in recent years, with Insomniac's Marvel's Spider-Man games showing how powerful a high-quality adaptation can be. Marvel's Wolverine is now giving Insomniac another major licensed character to tackle, and Warhorse Studios is working on an open-world Lord of the Rings RPG.

In other words, the industry isn't just throwing away the idea of licensed games anymore. Indiana Jones and the Great Circle also proved that a classic movie franchise can work in games when the project is built around what makes that property fun to inhabit. If studios are willing to take Spider-Man , Wolverine , Indiana Jones , and Lord of the Rings seriously, then Shrek shouldn't be limited to nostalgia memes and theater seats.

Of course, Shrek doesn't need the same kind of prestige-game treatment as those franchises either. The right Shrek game should probably be funnier, weirder, and more chaotic than most modern licensed games. That's why SuperSlam is such a good starting point, because it already knew Shrek was at its best when fairy-tale rules were being broken.

Even if DreamWorks never revives Shrek SuperSlam specifically, Shrek 5 should still make a new game easier to justify. The franchise has the characters, settings, humor, and nostalgia to support something more ambitious than a quick mobile release. A co-op adventure, party game, brawler, or open-area fairy-tale comedy game could all make sense if the studio behind it understood the tone.

The biggest point is that Shrek 's comeback should arguably feel bigger than one movie. Shrek 5 is already bringing the ogre back to the big screen after a long absence, and that alone will probably be enough to make longtime fans curious. But if DreamWorks wants Shrek 's return to feel like a true franchise revival, gaming deserves to be part of it, whatever that might look like.

Shrek 5

Animation
Comedy
Family
Fantasy
Release Date
June 30, 2027
Director
Conrad Vernon, Walt Dohrn, Brad Ableson
Writers
Michael McCullers, William Steig, Christopher Meledandri

Cast

See All
  • Mike Myers
    Shrek (voice)
  • Eddie Murphy
    Donkey (voice)
  • Cameron Diaz
    Princess Fiona (voice)
  • Antonio Banderas
    Puss In Boots (voice)

Producers
Andrew Adamson, Chris Meledandri, Gina Shay, Jeffrey Katzenberg
Franchise(s)
Shrek
Powered by

item.title: Destiny 2: How to Instantly Start Damage on Argos
item.description:
Argos is the first Pantheon 2.0 boss you'll face in Calus Resplendent, and he's also one of the easiest to beat. This mainly comes from the fact that you can cheese him by instantly skipping the setup and heading straight into the damage phase for a nice one-phase clear!

Skipping to damage phase against Argos in Destiny 2 is extremely easy, and we'll show you the quick process below so you can replicate it in every game you have going forward for those sweet Tier 5 loot drops.

Check out the guide below that will show you how to duplicate Artifact Perks, which can be used to gain enough power to one-phase bosses!

How to Instantly Start Damage Phase on Argos in Destiny 2

When you spawn into the Argos encounter, you're going to want to destroy three of the four Vex nodes sticking out of Argos . You need to leave the last one for now, as shooting it will activate Argos, and doing so will mean you can't skip to damage without a wipe.

When Argos has one remaining node to shoot, turn around and jump off the cliff onto a nearby ledge . When you're low enough (as shown in the spot from the image above), you can jump up to shoot the node in midair and land safely on the ledge.

When Argos spawns in via his mini-cutscene, you should get the text "Joining Allies". Once the timer hits zero, you will spawn back to the platform above , and Argos' shields will be lowered , which allows you to rush towards him and begin the DPS phase by destroying two of his open wounds .

Sometimes, Argos' shield is not lowered after joining allies. If this happens, try emoting during the countdown, or return to orbit and try again, as the instance might need to reset.

Argos isn't the only boss you can one-phase with ease. Check out our guides on how to perform the Titan Bubble Glitch to easily kill Morgeth and Insurrection Prime:

  • How to Cheese Morgeth
  • How to Cheese Insurrection Prime

Destiny 2

Follow
Followed
FPS
Systems
OpenCritic Reviews
Top Critic Avg: 84 /100 Critics Rec: 88%
Released
August 28, 2017
ESRB
T For TEEN for Blood, Language, and Violence
Developer(s)
Bungie
Publisher(s)
Bungie

WHERE TO PLAY

SUBSCRIPTION
DIGITAL
PHYSICAL
FX Wiki Wiki

Genre(s)
FPS
Platform(s)
PS5, PS4, Xbox Series X, Xbox Series S, Xbox One, PC, Stadia
Powered by
item.content:encoded:
Argos is the first Pantheon 2.0 boss you'll face in Calus Resplendent, and he's also one of the easiest to beat. This mainly comes from the fact that you can cheese him by instantly skipping the setup and heading straight into the damage phase for a nice one-phase clear!

Skipping to damage phase against Argos in Destiny 2 is extremely easy, and we'll show you the quick process below so you can replicate it in every game you have going forward for those sweet Tier 5 loot drops.

Check out the guide below that will show you how to duplicate Artifact Perks, which can be used to gain enough power to one-phase bosses!

How to Instantly Start Damage Phase on Argos in Destiny 2

When you spawn into the Argos encounter, you're going to want to destroy three of the four Vex nodes sticking out of Argos . You need to leave the last one for now, as shooting it will activate Argos, and doing so will mean you can't skip to damage without a wipe.

When Argos has one remaining node to shoot, turn around and jump off the cliff onto a nearby ledge . When you're low enough (as shown in the spot from the image above), you can jump up to shoot the node in midair and land safely on the ledge.

When Argos spawns in via his mini-cutscene, you should get the text "Joining Allies". Once the timer hits zero, you will spawn back to the platform above , and Argos' shields will be lowered , which allows you to rush towards him and begin the DPS phase by destroying two of his open wounds .

Sometimes, Argos' shield is not lowered after joining allies. If this happens, try emoting during the countdown, or return to orbit and try again, as the instance might need to reset.

Argos isn't the only boss you can one-phase with ease. Check out our guides on how to perform the Titan Bubble Glitch to easily kill Morgeth and Insurrection Prime:

  • How to Cheese Morgeth
  • How to Cheese Insurrection Prime
